{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016i4YjdAmZxvskgAfGe5tJb","type":"tool_result","content":"/app/.claude-harness/hooks/tbench_hooks.py\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"ec2a2077-f827-4e02-81ee-e372d0c75718","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"/app/.claude-harness/hooks/tbench_hooks.py\n#!/usr/bin/env \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_016BL6fvhGjU6VhJJA3yzCfj","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018epZfePZrBhpdZuBbwFcvo","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\n\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\nprint(f'X range: {min(xs):.2f} to {max(xs):.2f}')\nprint(f'Y range: {min(ys):.2f} to {max(ys):.2f}')\n\n# Find peaks near typical graphene Raman positions\nfor target in [1580, 2700]:\n    mask = (xs > target - 200) & (xs < target + 200)\n    if mask.any():\n        idx = np.where(mask)[0]\n        max_idx = idx[np.argmax(ys[mask])]\n        print(f'Peak near {target}: x={xs[max_idx]:.2f}, y={ys[max_idx]:.2f}')\n    else:\n        print(f'No data near {target}')\n\n# Look for actual peaks - find top 5 y values\ntop_idx = np.argsort(ys)[-10:][::-1]\nprint('Top 10 intensity points:')\nfor i in top_idx:\n    print(f'  x={xs[i]:.2f}, y={ys[i]:.2f}')\n\"\n","description":"Analyze data to find peaks"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":312,"cache_read_input_tokens":24524,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":312,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"e7fd04e2-a753-45e3-b5a4-28af586d7701"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_012DDD9zNszUDU1AeHNNpJbh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01F3yXFNiVjsGfuseYDcSRUW","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.signal import find_peaks\n\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\n# Find ALL peaks with different settings\npeaks, props = find_peaks(ys, prominence=1000, distance=10)\nprint(f'Peaks with prominence>1000: {len(peaks)}')\nfor p in peaks:\n    print(f'  x={xs[p]:.2f}, y={ys[p]:.2f}, prom={props[\\\"prominences\\\"][np.where(peaks==p)[0][0]]:.1f}')\n\nprint()\n# Also look at what the spectrum looks like in different regions\nregions = [(1600, 2000), (2000, 4000), (4000, 8000), (8000, 15000), (15000, 25000), (25000, 50000)]\nfor lo, hi in regions:\n    mask = (xs >= lo) & (xs <= hi)\n    if mask.any():\n        yvals = ys[mask]\n        xvals = xs[mask]\n        max_idx = np.argmax(yvals)\n        print(f'Region {lo}-{hi}: n={mask.sum()}, max_y={yvals[max_idx]:.1f} at x={xvals[max_idx]:.1f}, mean_y={yvals.mean():.1f}')\n\"\n","description":"Find all peaks and analyze spectrum regions"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1127,"cache_read_input_tokens":25549,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1127,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":10,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"ea5f6e39-20ac-49a0-9169-ff64dc7f0f67"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_015STu2pawFZCEqfx8Ffh5Nq","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01T4FBwXfaqoF4FahWfQvzEs","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\n\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\n# Check spacing pattern\ndiffs = np.diff(xs)\nprint(f'X step diffs: min={diffs.min():.4f}, max={diffs.max():.4f}')\nprint(f'First 5 diffs: {diffs[:5]}')\nprint(f'Middle 5 diffs: {diffs[1780:1785]}')\nprint(f'Last 5 diffs: {diffs[-5:]}')\n\n# X decreases - check 1/x to see if spacing is even in 1/x (wavelength)\ninv_xs = 1.0/xs\ninv_diffs = np.diff(inv_xs)\nprint(f'\\n1/X step diffs: min={inv_diffs.min():.10f}, max={inv_diffs.max():.10f}')\nprint(f'First 5: {inv_diffs[:5]}')\nprint(f'Middle 5: {inv_diffs[1780:1785]}')\nprint(f'Last 5: {inv_diffs[-5:]}')\n\n# Check if evenly spaced in some domain\n# If CCD pixels map to wavelength, the spacing in nm (or 1/cm^-1) would be uniform\nprint(f'\\nStd of 1/x diffs: {np.std(inv_diffs):.12f}')\nprint(f'Mean of 1/x diffs: {np.mean(inv_diffs):.12f}')\nprint(f'Coeff of variation: {np.std(inv_diffs)/np.abs(np.mean(inv_diffs)):.6f}')\n\nprint(f'\\nStd of x diffs: {np.std(diffs):.6f}')\nprint(f'Mean of x diffs: {np.mean(diffs):.6f}')\n\"\n","description":"Analyze x-axis spacing pattern"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3509,"cache_read_input_tokens":26676,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3509,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"db528f0e-22a0-4e70-abe1-6c6ca64ad630"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_017XvrjyEeX2qhPnFLcyxQhT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The spacing isn't uniform in either x or 1/x domain. The x-values decrease but with variable step size - the steps are larger at high x and smaller at low x. This is NOT consistent with a standard grating spectrometer (which would have roughly uniform spacing in wavelength or wavenumber).\n\nActually, looking at the spacing pattern:\n- At x~47000: step ~-595\n- At x~2578: step ~-1.03\n- At x~1649: step ~-0.26\n\nThe step size seems proportional to x² (or some power of x). This is consistent with x being in wavenumber (cm⁻¹) if the detector measures uniformly spaced wavelengths.\n \nIf the detector has pixels uniformly spaced in wavelength λ, and x = 1/λ (wavenumber), then dx/dλ = -x², which perfectly explains why the steps scale with x². So x is definitely in wavenumber, spanning from 1649 to 47184 cm⁻¹. Now I need to check what this range means for graphene Raman spectroscopy with a 532 nm laser.\n\nThe laser wavenumber would be around 18797 cm⁻¹, placing the Rayleigh peak there, with the G peak at 17217 cm⁻¹ and the 2D peak at 16097 cm⁻¹. The massive peak at x=19140 is suspiciously close to this, though it would correspond to a 522.5 nm laser, which isn't standard.\n\nActually, let me reconsider—the peak at 19140 is 343 cm⁻¹ away from the expected 532 nm laser position, putting it on the anti-Stokes side. Maybe I should check if a different common Raman laser wavelength fits better, like 785 nm, 633 nm, 514 nm, or 488 nm. I'm realizing I should stop overthinking the wavelength conversions and just focus on what the problem actually asks—identifying and fitting the G and 2D peaks in the spectrum. Looking at the data, that prominent peak at 19140 with intensity 79400 is clearly the dominant feature, so I need to examine the spectrum more carefully to see if there's a distinct second peak that would correspond to the 2D band. The peak at 19140 would be deep in the UV if this were actually Raman shift in cm⁻¹, which doesn't make physical sense. Let me set aside trying to interpret the units and just focus on fitting the two most prominent peaks in the data—the massive one around x≈19140 and the next largest around x≈3745—since those are what the task is asking me to identify as the G and 2D peaks. Looking at the regional analysis, there's a sharp peak at x=3745 with y=12921 standing well above the background in the 2000-4000 range, while the 1600-2000 region shows elevated values around 1660 that might be part of a broader feature. I'm realizing the data is ordered from high to low x values, which changes how I should interpret the spectrum. Maybe the instrument is an FT-Raman or uses a different convention altogether. I think the most straightforward explanation is that the x-axis represents Raman shift in cm⁻¹, the data spans a broad range, and that peak around 19140 is likely an artifact or the Rayleigh line rather than an actual Raman peak. The G and 2D peaks for graphene should appear near 1580 and 2700 cm⁻¹, but the data only starts at 1649, so the G peak might be just outside the range. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01NwsVeJbmZzuAYQHY6jAs6p","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HjhCUTPoLjmKCBAVA52LtN","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.signal import find_peaks\n\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\n# Reverse to have increasing x for easier analysis\nxs = xs[::-1]\nys = ys[::-1]\n\n# Find all peaks  \npeaks, props = find_peaks(ys, prominence=500, distance=5)\nprint('All peaks with prominence > 500:')\nfor i, p in enumerate(peaks):\n    # Estimate peak width\n    half_max = (ys[p] + props['prominences'][i]*0.0) / 2 + props['prominences'][i]*0.0\n    print(f'  x={xs[p]:.2f}, y={ys[p]:.2f}, prom={props[\\\"prominences\\\"][i]:.1f}')\n\n# Focus on the 6 most prominent peaks and estimate their widths\nfrom scipy.signal import peak_widths\nwidths, width_heights, left_ips, right_ips = peak_widths(ys, peaks, rel_height=0.5)\nprint(f'\\nPeaks with estimated widths (in data points):')\norder = np.argsort(props['prominences'])[::-1]\nfor rank, i in enumerate(order[:8]):\n    p = peaks[i]\n    w = widths[i]\n    # Estimate width in x units\n    if p > 0 and p < len(xs)-1:\n        dx = abs(xs[p+1] - xs[p-1])/2\n        wx = w * dx\n    else:\n        wx = 0\n    print(f'  #{rank+1}: x={xs[p]:.2f}, y={ys[p]:.2f}, prom={props[\\\"prominences\\\"][i]:.1f}, width_pts={w:.1f}, width_x≈{wx:.1f}')\n\"\n","description":"Analyze peak widths to distinguish narrow Raman peaks from broad features"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2047,"cache_read_input_tokens":35927,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2047,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"864530d9-438e-4f69-bbd3-89e22f72ce11"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01SZZyrYpi4aRpSZeFQqPXAG","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Xyu5w9JnZCP7rhFi7Q92wi","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import curve_fit\n\n# Load data\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\n# Verify: 10^7/peak_x gives Raman shift\npeaks_x = [19139.54, 10289.94, 6329.37, 3745.05]\nprint('Peak positions and corresponding Raman shifts (10^7/x):')\nfor px in peaks_x:\n    print(f'  x={px:.2f} -> shift={1e7/px:.1f} cm^-1')\nprint()\n\n# Lorentzian function\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return offset + amplitude * gamma**2 / ((x - x0)**2 + gamma**2)\n\n# Fit G peak (x ~ 6329, Raman shift ~ 1580 cm^-1)\ng_mask = (xs > 5800) & (xs < 7200)\ng_x = xs[g_mask]\ng_y = ys[g_mask]\n# Sort by x\nsort_idx = np.argsort(g_x)\ng_x = g_x[sort_idx]\ng_y = g_y[sort_idx]\n\np0_g = [6329, 50, 13000, 4000]  # initial guess\ntry:\n    popt_g, pcov_g = curve_fit(lorentzian, g_x, g_y, p0=p0_g, maxfev=10000)\n    print('G peak fit:')\n    print(f'  x0 = {popt_g[0]:.6f}')\n    print(f'  gamma = {popt_g[1]:.6f}')\n    print(f'  amplitude = {popt_g[2]:.6f}')\n    print(f'  offset = {popt_g[3]:.6f}')\n    # Check fit quality\n    g_pred = lorentzian(g_x, *popt_g)\n    residual = np.sqrt(np.mean((g_y - g_pred)**2))\n    print(f'  RMSE = {residual:.2f}')\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f'G fit error: {e}')\n\nprint()\n\n# Fit 2D peak (x ~ 3745, Raman shift ~ 2670 cm^-1)\nd2_mask = (xs > 3400) & (xs < 4200)\nd2_x = xs[d2_mask]\nd2_y = ys[d2_mask]\nsort_idx = np.argsort(d2_x)\nd2_x = d2_x[sort_idx]\nd2_y = d2_y[sort_idx]\n\np0_2d = [3745, 30, 12000, 1500]\ntry:\n    popt_2d, pcov_2d = curve_fit(lorentzian, d2_x, d2_y, p0=p0_2d, maxfev=10000)\n    print('2D peak fit:')\n    print(f'  x0 = {popt_2d[0]:.6f}')\n    print(f'  gamma = {popt_2d[1]:.6f}')\n    print(f'  amplitude = {popt_2d[2]:.6f}')\n    print(f'  offset = {popt_2d[3]:.6f}')\n    d2_pred = lorentzian(d2_x, *popt_2d)\n    residual = np.sqrt(np.mean((d2_y - d2_pred)**2))\n    print(f'  RMSE = {residual:.2f}')\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f'2D fit error: {e}')\n\"\n","description":"Fit Lorentzian to G and 2D peaks"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3678,"cache_read_input_tokens":37974,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3678,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"760b2cfb-8638-42e9-8a02-3b0ae8033712"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_017NpKs3Ue9oJffbRjMjZZvs","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GTykCugtRV4Pu5ZZbEKZGx","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 -c \"\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import curve_fit\n\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return offset + amplitude * gamma**2 / ((x - x0)**2 + gamma**2)\n\n# Try different window sizes to verify stability\nfor g_lo, g_hi, label in [(5800, 7200, 'wide'), (6000, 6800, 'narrow'), (5500, 7500, 'wider')]:\n    mask = (xs > g_lo) & (xs < g_hi)\n    xf = xs[mask]; yf = ys[mask]\n    idx = np.argsort(xf); xf = xf[idx]; yf = yf[idx]\n    try:\n        popt, _ = curve_fit(lorentzian, xf, yf, p0=[3745, 30, 12000, 1500], maxfev=10000)\n        print(f'2D [{label}]: x0={popt[0]:.2f} gamma={popt[1]:.2f} amp={popt[2]:.2f} off={popt[3]:.2f}')\n    except: print(f'2D [{label}]: failed')\n\"\n","description":"Verify fit stability with different window sizes"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4095,"cache_read_input_tokens":41652,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4095,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"6f10371e-1950-4da4-b531-75eee632a5d7"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_015HF6GQVtTsC8mfyz4aW9ud","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01JQSpWoTtvSbzz5xNnax8Cc","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'PYEOF'\nimport json\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy.optimize import curve_fit\n\n# Load data\nxs = []\nys = []\nwith open('/app/graphene.dat') as f:\n    for line in f:\n        parts = line.strip().split('\\t')\n        x = float(parts[0].replace(',', '.'))\n        y = float(parts[1].replace(',', '.'))\n        xs.append(x)\n        ys.append(y)\n\nxs = np.array(xs)\nys = np.array(ys)\n\n# Lorentzian: y = offset + amplitude * gamma^2 / ((x-x0)^2 + gamma^2)\ndef lorentzian(x, x0, gamma, amplitude, offset):\n    return offset + amplitude * gamma**2 / ((x - x0)**2 + gamma**2)\n\n# Fit G peak (x ~ 6329)\ng_mask = (xs > 5800) & (xs < 7200)\ng_x = xs[g_mask]; g_y = ys[g_mask]\nidx = np.argsort(g_x); g_x = g_x[idx]; g_y = g_y[idx]\npopt_g, _ = curve_fit(lorentzian, g_x, g_y, p0=[6329, 50, 13000, 4000], maxfev=10000)\n\n# Fit 2D peak (x ~ 3745)\nd2_mask = (xs > 3400) & (xs < 4200)\nd2_x = xs[d2_mask]; d2_y = ys[d2_mask]\nidx = np.argsort(d2_x); d2_x = d2_x[idx]; d2_y = d2_y[idx]\npopt_2d, _ = curve_fit(lorentzian, d2_x, d2_y, p0=[3745, 30, 12000, 1500], maxfev=10000)\n\nresults = {\n    \"G\": {\n        \"x0\": round(float(popt_g[0]), 6),\n        \"gamma\": round(float(abs(popt_g[1])), 6),\n        \"amplitude\": round(float(popt_g[2]), 6),\n        \"offset\": round(float(popt_g[3]), 6)\n    },\n    \"2D\": {\n        \"x0\": round(float(popt_2d[0]), 6),\n        \"gamma\": round(float(abs(popt_2d[1])), 6),\n        \"amplitude\": round(float(popt_2d[2]), 6),\n        \"offset\": round(float(popt_2d[3]), 6)\n    }\n}\n\nwith open('/app/results.json', 'w') as f:\n    json.dump(results, f, indent=2)\n\nprint(json.dumps(results, indent=2))\nprint(\"\\nWritten to /app/results.json\")\nPYEOF","description":"Generate final results and write to results.json"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":804,"cache_read_input_tokens":47418,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":804,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2602391e-f18d-4698-9daf-6ff079c1e296","uuid":"014c0d3d-9aa7-4aea-a0f0-133566cda460"}
